title: Eye Floaters Be Gone: 10 Powerful Herbal Tinctures

remedy_constituents.constituent_desc: Quercetin is good for eye floaters because it has anti-inflammatory properties that can reduce swelling and irritation in the eyes, helping to prevent and treat floaters caused by inflammation or damage to the vitreous gel in the eye.
Kaempferol is good for eye floaters because it has anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce the floaters' visibility by reducing the inflammation in the eyes.
Isorhapontigenin is good for eye floaters because it has anti-inflammatory properties that may help reduce the formation of blood cells in the vitreous gel in the eye, which can cause eye floaters.

remedies.remedy_desc: Ginkgo biloba tincture is a herbal remedy that may help reduce eye floaters. Eye floaters are small, dark spots that appear in your field of vision due to age-related changes in the vitreous gel inside your eye. This gel becomes cloudy and breaks down, causing the floaters to appear. Ginkgo biloba tincture is believed to improve blood flow to the eyes, which may help reduce inflammation and clear out the cloudy vitreous gel. This, in turn, may help reduce the appearance of eye floaters. Some people also use Ginkgo biloba tincture to improve their overall eye health and reduce the risk of age-related macular degeneration, a condition that can cause vision loss. Ginkgo biloba tincture is often taken by mouth, and it's recommended to start with a small dose and gradually increase as needed.
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make Avena sativa herbal tincture that helps with eye floaters, start by gathering the necessary materials: dried Avena sativa root, a clean glass jar with a lid, a sharp knife, a coffee grinder or mortar and pestle, and a neutral liquid like glycerin or vodka. Begin by cutting the dried root into small pieces and then grind them into a fine powder using the coffee grinder or mortar and pestle. Next, fill the glass jar about one-third full with the powdered root. Pour the neutral liquid over the root, making sure that the liquid covers all the powder. Stir the mixture well to ensure the root and liquid are fully combined. Seal the jar with the lid and let it sit in a cool, dark place for about two weeks. This will allow the liquid to extract the medicinal properties from the root. After two weeks, strain the liquid through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another clean glass jar. Discard the solids and store the Avena sativa tincture in a dark place. Take a few drops of the tincture as needed, usually in the morning and evening, and mix it with water or another liquid to take internally.
remedies.image_alt: Ginkgo biloba herbal tinctures for eye floaters

remedy_constituents.constituent_desc: Bacosides are good for eye floaters because they help to strengthen the blood vessels in the retina and improve the overall health of the eyes, which can reduce the appearance of eye floaters and improve vision clarity.
Flavonoids are good for eye floaters because they have antioxidant properties that help reduce inflammation and oxidative stress in the eye, which can cause floaters to appear more visible.
Bacoside A is good for eye floaters because it has anti-inflammatory properties that can reduce the appearance of floaters by repairing and protecting the delicate blood vessels in the eyes.

remedies.remedy_desc: Bacopa monnieri tincture is a natural remedy that may help people with eye floaters. Eye floaters are tiny spots that appear in your vision due to a gel-like substance called vitreous detachment in your eye. They can be caused by aging, eye injuries, or medical conditions. Bacopa monnieri is an herb that has been used in Ayurvedic medicine for centuries to improve memory and cognitive function. It contains compounds called bacosides, which may help repair and strengthen the vitreous gel in the eye, reducing the number of floaters. Some studies suggest that Bacopa monnieri may also improve blood flow to the eyes, which can help to clear out debris and reduce floaters. Additionally, Bacopa monnieri tincture has anti-inflammatory properties, which may help to reduce swelling and promote healing in the eyes. Taking Bacopa monnieri tincture may be a good option for people who want to try a natural remedy for eye floaters before turning to surgery or other medical treatments.
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make Avena sativa herbal tincture for eye floaters, you will need Avena sativa seeds or dried leaves, high-proof vodka, a clean glass jar with a lid, and a cheesecloth or a coffee filter. First, dry the Avena sativa seeds or leaves in a low-temperature oven or by air-drying them in a cool, dark place. Once dry, transfer the Avena sativa to the glass jar and cover it with vodka, making sure the herb is completely submerged. Seal the jar and let it sit in a cool, dark place for 2-3 weeks, shaking the jar every few days to help the vodka extract the herb's properties. After the extraction period, strain the liquid through the cheesecloth or coffee filter into another glass container, discarding the solids. Store the Avena sativa tincture in the refrigerator to slow down the aging process and use it as directed by a healthcare professional, typically by placing a few drops under the tongue or adding it to water.
remedies.image_alt: Bacopa monnieri herbal tinctures for eye floaters

remedy_constituents.constituent_desc: Asiaticoside is good for eye floaters because it helps to improve blood circulation and reduce inflammation in the eyes, which can make eye floaters less noticeable and even disappear over time.
Madecassoside is good for eye floaters because it helps to reduce inflammation and improve blood flow to the eyes, which can help to clear away debris that causes eye floaters to appear.

Tannins are good for eye floaters because they have anti-inflammatory properties that can reduce swelling and improve blood flow to the retina, helping to clear away the small debris that causes eye floaters.

remedies.remedy_desc: Centella asiatica tincture is used to help people with eye floaters. Eye floaters are small spots or threads that can appear in our vision, often caused by changes in the jelly inside the eye. The tincture is made from the plant Centella asiatica, also known as Gotu kola. This plant is known for its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. When taken as a tincture, it may help reduce inflammation in the eye and improve blood flow. This can lead to the clearing of eye floaters, as the jelly inside the eye can repair and restore itself. The tincture may also help improve vision clarity by reducing the appearance of floaters and other vision disturbances. Some people take Centella asiatica tincture in addition to other treatments, such as eye exercises and dietary changes, to help manage eye floaters.
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make Avena sativa herbal tincture for eye floaters, start by harvesting or purchasing dried Avena sativa root. You will need a clean glass jar with a lid and a carrier oil like olive or grapeseed oil. Fill the jar about 1/4 full with the dried root, then cover it completely with the carrier oil. Store the jar in a cool, dark place for 4-6 weeks, shaking it every day or two to help the plant's goodness mix with the oil. After the steeping period, use cheesecloth or a coffee filter to strain the mixture into another clean glass bottle, discarding the solids. The liquid left is your Avena sativa tincture, which you can use sublingually (under the tongue) or add to water to drink. To make a stronger tincture, simply repeat the process with fresh root and oil, then combine the two batches. Keep in mind that it's essential to consult with a healthcare professional before using any herbal remedy, especially if you're experiencing eye problems or are pregnant/breastfeeding.
remedies.image_alt: Centella asiatica herbal tinctures for eye floaters

remedy_constituents.constituent_desc: Curcumin is good for eye floaters because it has anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce eye pressure and promote healthy blood flow to the retina, which can help improve vision and reduce the appearance of eye floaters.
Demethoxycurcumin is good for eye floaters because it has anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce the growth of vitreous gel clots in the eye, which can cause floaters.

Polyphenols are good for eye floaters because they have antioxidant properties that help reduce inflammation and improve blood flow to the eyes, which may help clear out debris that causes eye floaters.

remedies.remedy_desc: Curcuma longa tincture is made from the root of the turmeric plant. This plant has been used for centuries in traditional medicine, especially in Ayurvedic practices. Turmeric contains a powerful compound called curcumin, which is known for its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. When it comes to eye floaters, inflammation and oxidative stress in the eyes can cause tiny pieces of the eye's gel-like substance called vitreous to break loose and float in our vision. Curcuma longa tincture may help reduce eye inflammation and oxidative stress, which in turn may help reduce the number of eye floaters. This is because curcumin in the tincture can block pro-inflammatory enzymes and free radicals that contribute to eye inflammation and damage. Some people have reported improvements in their eye floaters after using Curcuma longa tincture, possibly due to its ability to improve blood flow and reduce inflammation in the eyes.
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make Avena sativa herbal tincture that may help with eye floaters, start by gathering the necessary ingredients: dried Avena sativa root, a clean glass jar, and a high-proof vodka or glycerin. Begin by filling the glass jar with 1 part of dried Avena sativa root and then add 2 parts of vodka or glycerin. For example, use 1 ounce of dried root and 2 ounces of vodka. Next, seal the jar tightly and store it in a cool, dark place for 2-4 weeks, shaking the jar every day to help the herbs infuse with the liquid. This process is called maceration, which helps to extract the active compounds from the herbs. After the maceration period, strain the liquid through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another clean glass jar, discarding the solids. The resulting liquid is the Avena sativa herbal tincture, which can be taken sublingually, or under the tongue, to help alleviate eye floaters. Start with a small dose of 5-10 drops and gradually increase as needed, under the guidance of a healthcare professional.
remedies.image_alt: Curcuma longa herbal tinctures for eye floaters

remedy_constituents.constituent_desc: Quercetin is good for eye floaters because it has anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce swelling and improve vision by reducing the appearance of floaters.
Resveratrol helps to reduce eye floaters by protecting the tiny blood vessels in the eyes from damage and inflammation, which can cause floaters to appear, and by improving blood flow to the retina.
Kaempferol is good for eye floaters because it has antioxidant properties that help reduce inflammation and improve blood vessel health in the eyes, which can reduce the visibility of eye floaters.
Catechin is good for eye floaters because it has anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce swelling and improve vision clarity by reducing the appearance of floaters.

Tannins are good for eye floaters because they have anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce swelling in the blood vessels of the eyes, allowing the tiny particles that cause floaters to move out of the way.

remedies.remedy_desc: Vitis vinifera tincture is a liquid extract made from the grapevine plant. It's been used for centuries in traditional medicine to improve eye health. The tannins and flavonoids in the grapevine plant have anti-inflammatory properties that may help reduce eye inflammation and swelling. This can be beneficial for people experiencing eye floaters, which are small spots that appear in the field of vision due to tiny debris in the eye's vitreous humor. The anti-inflammatory properties of Vitis vinifera tincture may help calm the inflammation and reduce the appearance of eye floaters. Some users also report that Vitis vinifera tincture improves blood flow to the eyes, which may help the eye's natural cleaning process remove debris and reduce floaters.
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make Avena sativa herbal tincture that may help with eye floaters, you'll need dried Avena sativa root, a clean glass jar, a bottle of vodka or another high-proof alcohol, and a dark glass dropper bottle. First, measure out a handful of dried Avena sativa root and place it in the clean glass jar. Then, pour enough vodka over the herbs to cover them completely, making sure the root is fully submerged. Seal the jar and let it sit in a cool, dark place for 2-6 weeks, shaking the jar occasionally to help the herbs infuse into the vodka. This process is called maceration, and it allows the herbal properties to transfer into the alcohol. After the infusion period, strain the liquid through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into the dark glass dropper bottle, discarding the solids. Store the tincture in a cool, dark place and use 20-30 drops under the tongue as needed to support eye health and potentially reduce eye floaters.

remedy_constituents.constituent_desc: Gingerol is good for eye floaters because it has anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce swelling and pressure in the eyes, which may help clear up or reduce the visibility of the tiny particles causing the floaters.
Shogaol is good for eye floaters because it helps to increase blood flow to the eyes and reduce inflammation, which can help to clear out debris and reduce the visibility of floaters.
Polyphenols have anti-inflammatory properties that may help reduce the appearance of eye floaters by reducing inflammation in the eyes and improving blood flow.
Shogaols are good for eye floaters because they have anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce the appearance of floaters by reducing swelling and irritation in the eyes.

remedies.remedy_desc: Zingiber officinale, also known as ginger, is a natural remedy that may help with eye floaters. Eye floaters are tiny spots that appear in your vision due to small clumps of cells in the eye's vitreous gel. These cells can be caused by aging, injury, or inflammation. Ginger tincture, made from the root of the ginger plant, has anti-inflammatory properties that may help reduce inflammation in the eye. This could potentially help clear the small cells and reduce the appearance of eye floaters. Additionally, ginger has antioxidant properties that may help protect the eyes from damage caused by free radicals, which can contribute to the formation of eye floaters. Some people have reported improved vision and reduced eye floaters after taking ginger tincture regularly. However, it's essential to consult with a healthcare professional before trying any new remedies, especially if you have a pre-existing eye condition.
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make Avena sativa herbal tincture that may help with eye floaters, start by gathering dried Avena sativa flowers, a clean glass jar with a lid, and a high-proof solvent like vodka or glycerin. Begin by measuring 1 part of dried Avena sativa flowers to 2 parts of solvent, filling the jar with the mixture. Seal the jar and place it in a cool, dark place for 2-3 weeks, shaking the jar every day. This process is called maceration, where the solvent extracts the beneficial properties from the herbs. After the maceration period, strain the liquid through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another clean glass bottle, discarding the solids. Store the tincture in a cool, dark place and take 20-30 drops, 2-3 times a day, by mixing it with water or taking it sublingually. It's essential to consult with a healthcare professional before using any herbal remedy, especially if you have pre-existing medical conditions or are taking medications.

remedy_constituents.constituent_desc: Baicalein is good for eye floaters because it has anti-inflammatory properties that can help to reduce the inflammation and swelling in the eyes that can cause floaters to appear.
Baicalin helps eye floaters by reducing inflammation in the eyes, which can cause floaters, and also improving blood flow to the retina to promote healthy vision.

Wogonin is good for eye floaters because it may help reduce inflammation in the eyes and improve vision clarity by blocking the production of a protein that causes blood vessel growth in the eye, which can contribute to the formation of eye floaters.

remedies.remedy_desc: Scutellaria baicalensis tincture is a natural remedy that may help reduce eye floaters. Eye floaters are small, dark spots that appear in your vision due to age-related changes in the gel inside the eyeball. They can be caused by tiny clumps of collagen that break loose and float in the vitreous gel. Scutellaria baicalensis, also known as Chinese skullcap, has anti-inflammatory properties that may help to reduce the inflammation and promote the clearing of these collagen clumps. The tincture can be applied topically to the eyes or taken orally to help reduce inflammation and promote eye health. Some people have reported a decrease in eye floaters after taking Scutellaria baicalensis tincture, possibly due to its ability to reduce inflammation and promote the natural healing process of the eye.
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make Avena sativa herbal tinctures that help with eye floaters, start by gathering high-quality dried Avena sativa root and a neutral carrier oil such as sunflower or grapeseed oil. Combine one part of dried Avena sativa root with two parts of the carrier oil in a clean glass jar, making sure the root is completely covered by the oil. Store the jar in a cool, dark place and let it sit for 2-3 weeks, shaking the jar every few days to help the roots infuse into the oil. After the infusion period, strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another clean glass jar, discarding the solids. Transfer the tincture to a dark glass bottle and add a few drops of vitamin E oil to help preserve the tincture. You can take the Avena sativa tincture by adding a few drops to water or another liquid, and it's recommended to start with a small dose and gradually increase as needed. It's essential to note that Avena sativa tincture is not a replacement for medical treatment, and if you experience persistent or severe eye floaters, you should consult with an eye care professional for proper diagnosis and treatment.

remedies.remedy_desc: Panax ginseng tincture is a herbal remedy that may help reduce eye floaters. Eye floaters are small, dark spots that appear in your field of vision due to age-related changes in the vitreous gel inside your eye. This gel can break down and form clumps, casting shadows on the retina and causing floaters. Panax ginseng tincture is made from the roots of the ginseng plant, which is rich in antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds. These compounds may help reduce inflammation and improve circulation in the eyes, which can help break up the clumps of vitreous gel and reduce the appearance of eye floaters. Additionally, the antioxidants in Panax ginseng tincture may help protect the eyes from damage caused by free radicals, which can contribute to the development of eye floaters. Some people may find that taking Panax ginseng tincture regularly helps alleviate the symptoms of eye floaters, but it's essential to note that individual results may vary.
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make Avena sativa herbal tinctures that may help with eye floaters, you'll need a few simple ingredients and some basic equipment. Start by gathering fresh or dried Avena sativa plant material, which can be found at health food stores or online. Next, combine 1 part of the Avena sativa plant material with 2 parts of a neutral solvent, such as vodka or glycerin, in a clean glass jar. Seal the jar and store it in a cool, dark place for 2-6 weeks, shaking the jar every day or two to help the solvent extract the beneficial compounds from the plant material. After the extraction period, strain the liquid through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another clean glass container, discarding the solids. Transfer the tincture to a dark glass dropper bottle and label it for future use. It's recommended to take 20-30 drops of the Avena sativa tincture, 2-3 times a day, mixed with water or taken sublingually (under the tongue), to help alleviate eye floaters and promote overall eye health.

remedies.remedy_desc: Viscum album tincture is a herbal remedy made from the mistletoe plant. It's been used for centuries to improve eye health. Some people find that it helps to reduce eye floaters, which are small spots that appear in your field of vision. These spots are usually harmless but can be annoying and affect your vision. Viscum album tincture works by increasing blood flow to the eyes and reducing inflammation. This helps to clear out any debris or damage in the eyes that may be causing the floaters. The tincture also contains antioxidants, which help to protect the eyes from damage and promote healing. Some users report that taking Viscum album tincture regularly helps to improve their eye health and reduce the appearance of floaters. It's often taken in small doses, 2-3 times a day, and can be combined with other herbal remedies to enhance its effects.
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make Avena sativa herbal tinctures for eye floaters, start by harvesting or purchasing dried Avena sativa root. This herb is also known as oat straw or milky oats, but for tincture-making, the root is preferred. Next, combine the dried root in a clean glass jar with a 1:5 ratio of herb to solvent, usually 80-100 proof vodka. For example, use one part Avena sativa root to five parts vodka. Fill the jar completely, making sure the herb is completely covered by the solvent. Seal the jar tightly and place it in a cool, dark place for 2-6 weeks, shaking the jar every day or two. This process allows the herb's active compounds to infuse into the solvent, creating a strong herbal extract. After the infusion period, strain the liquid through a cheesecloth or coffee filter into another clean glass bottle, discarding the solids. Label and store the tincture in a cool, dark place, ready to use as a supplement to support eye health and help reduce eye floaters.

remedies.remedy_desc: Avena sativa tincture is a natural remedy that may help reduce eye floaters. Eye floaters are tiny spots that appear in your vision due to age-related changes in the vitreous gel inside your eye. Avena sativa, also known as oat straw, contains a group of nutrients and antioxidants that help calm and soothe the eyes. The tincture is made by steeping the oat straw in a liquid and then straining it to extract its active ingredients. When taken internally, Avena sativa tincture may help reduce inflammation and improve circulation to the eyes. This, in turn, may help break down and clear out the tiny debris that causes eye floaters. Additionally, Avena sativa has anti-inflammatory properties that may help reduce eye strain and stress, which can contribute to eye floaters. Some people find that taking Avena sativa tincture regularly helps reduce the visibility of eye floaters and improves their overall eye health.
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make Avena sativa herbal tincture for eye floaters, you'll need dried Avena sativa, or oat straw, a clean glass jar, and a neutral spirit like vodka or glycerin. Start by measuring out a cup of dried Avena sativa and placing it in the glass jar. Next, fill the jar with the neutral spirit, making sure that the plant material is completely covered. Secure the lid tightly and place the jar in a cool, dark place for two to three weeks, shaking the jar every day to help the plant material infuse into the liquid. This process is called maceration and allows the active compounds in the Avena sativa to extract into the spirit. After the maceration period, strain the liquid through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another clean glass jar, discarding the solids. Finally, store the tincture in a cool, dark place and use it as directed - typically 20 to 30 drops in water or under the tongue, two to three times a day.

intro_desc: Eye floaters can be a frustrating and concerning issue, causing you to see small spots or threads floating in your vision. But there's good news - herbal tinctures can be a natural and effective way to address this problem. One key herb is Ginkgo biloba, which has anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce the occurrence of eye floaters. Ginkgo biloba improves blood flow to the eyes, which in turn reduces inflammation and helps to break down the vitreous gel that can cause floaters. Bacopa monnieri is another herb that has been shown to be beneficial in reducing eye floaters. It has antioxidant properties that can help protect the eyes from damage and reduce inflammation.  It's essential to fix the issue of eye floaters because it can significantly impact your daily life. Imagine being unable to read, drive, or watch TV due to distracting floaters in your vision. This can lead to feelings of anxiety, stress, and frustration, affecting your overall quality of life. Additionally, eye floaters can be a sign of an underlying issue, such as age-related macular degeneration or a detached retina, which can have serious consequences if left untreated.  Using herbal tinctures like Ginkgo biloba and Bacopa monnieri can help alleviate eye floaters and improve your vision. These herbs work together to reduce inflammation, improve blood flow, and protect the eyes from damage. By incorporating herbal tinctures into your daily routine, you can take a proactive approach to addressing eye floaters and improving your overall eye health.
supplementary_best: For eye floaters, a combination of herbal tinctures may help alleviate symptoms. Ginkgo biloba and Centella asiatica are chosen due to their ability to improve blood circulation to the eyes, which may reduce the appearance of floaters. Ginkgo biloba has anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce swelling and inflammation in the eyes, while Centella asiatica can improve vision clarity and eye health.  Bacopa monnieri is added to enhance brain function and memory, which may help the brain adapt to changes in vision. This herb may also reduce stress and anxiety related to the appearance of floaters.  Curcuma longa (turmeric) has potent anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce inflammation and oxidative stress in the eyes. Its antioxidant properties may also help protect the eyes from further damage.  The combination of these herbs may provide a synergistic effect in reducing eye floaters.
supplementary_avoid: If you have eye floaters, it's best to avoid herbal tinctures that can increase bleeding or worsen blood vessel damage. One such herb is Danshen (Salvia miltiorrhiza), which can thin the blood and increase the risk of bleeding. Another herb to avoid is Cinnamon (Cinnamomum verum), which may worsen blood vessel damage and increase the risk of bleeding in people with eye floaters.  You should also be cautious with herbs that can increase blood pressure or worsen eye problems. Yohimbe (Pausinystalia johimbe) can increase blood pressure, which may worsen eye floaters. Similarly, Ephedra (Ephedra sinica) and Ma Huang (Ephedra sinica) can also increase blood pressure and worsen eye problems.  Additionally, herbs that can cause vision problems or worsen eye conditions should be avoided. Black cohosh (Actaea racemosa) can cause vision problems, including blurred vision, and worsen eye conditions. Blue cohosh (Caulophyllum thalictroides) can also cause vision problems and worsen eye conditions, particularly eye floaters.  It's essential to consult with a healthcare professional before taking any herbal tinctures, especially if you have eye floaters. They can help you choose safe and effective herbal remedies that won't worsen your condition.
supplementary_other_remedies: Besides herbal tinctures, other natural remedies that can help with eye floaters include eating foods rich in omega-3 fatty acids. These healthy fats, found in fish oil and flaxseed oil, help reduce inflammation in the eyes and may help clear away floaters. Some people also try acupuncture to improve blood flow to the eyes, which can help reduce the appearance of floaters. Additionally, getting enough vitamin B6 and B12 in the diet can help reduce the severity of eye floaters. These vitamins help maintain healthy blood vessels, which can help clear away debris that causes floaters. Other remedies include taking eye exercises and relaxation techniques, such as meditation and deep breathing, to reduce eye strain and promote overall eye health. Some people also try getting more vitamin C in their diet, which can help improve the clarity of vision and reduce the appearance of floaters.
supplementary_related_ailments: People who experience eye floaters may also have other health issues that can be helped with medicinal herbs. Dry eyes, for example, can be caused by a lack of omega-3 fatty acids in the diet. The herb flaxseed contains omega-3, which can help to reduce inflammation and promote healthy tear production.  Inflammation in the body is often linked to eye floaters, as it can cause the blood vessels in the eye to become fragile and break, leading to the appearance of floaters. Turmeric contains a compound called curcumin, which has potent anti-inflammatory properties that can help to reduce swelling and promote healing in the body.  Some people who experience eye floaters may also have high blood pressure, which can cause the blood vessels in the eye to become damaged and lead to the appearance of floaters. Hawthorn is a herb that can help to lower blood pressure by relaxing the blood vessels and improving blood flow.  Additionally, people who experience eye floaters may also have a lack of antioxidants in their diet, which can contribute to the development of floaters. Bilberry is a herb that is rich in antioxidants, which can help to protect the eyes from damage and promote healthy vision.  People who experience eye floaters may also have a vitamin deficiency, such as a lack of vitamin B12. Vitamin B12 plays an important role in the health of the nervous system, and a deficiency can cause a range of symptoms including eye problems. The herb spirulina is a rich source of vitamin B12 and can help to promote healthy nerve function.  Finally, people who experience eye floaters may also have a lack of omega-6 fatty acids in their diet, which can contribute to inflammation in the body. Evening primrose oil is a herb that is rich in omega-6 fatty acids and can help to reduce inflammation and promote healthy vision.
